title: Chargeback reason code catalogue
description: Every supported reason code across every network + its canonical category + human-readable description + dispute time limits. The valid_reason_code rule reads this catalogue. RefundObserver + ChargebackObserver both use it to freeze reason_category + reason_description at chargeback creation.

reasons.display_name: Visa 10.1 — EMV Liability Shift Counterfeit Fraud
reasons.description: Card-present transaction where a counterfeit chip card was used without EMV. Merchant liable per EMV liability shift.

reasons.display_name: Visa 10.2 — EMV Liability Shift Non-Counterfeit Fraud
reasons.description: Card-present transaction with a lost/stolen chip card where EMV was not used. Merchant liable per EMV liability shift.
Card-present fraud that doesn't fit other codes. Rare.

Cardholder claims a card-absent transaction was not authorized by them. Most common online-fraud chargeback reason. CE3.0-eligible when merchant has prior legitimate purchase history from same customer.
Fraud identified via Visa's fraud monitoring program. Merchant enrolled in VFMP.
Transaction processed despite the card being flagged for recovery.
Transaction was declined by the issuer but processed anyway. Merchant liable.
Transaction processed without obtaining authorization.
Transaction presented outside the authorized time window (typically 30 days for card-present, 180 days for card-absent).
Wrong transaction type processed.
Transaction processed in wrong currency.
Transaction posted to wrong account number.
Transaction amount does not match the authorized amount.
Same transaction posted multiple times.
Cardholder paid for the same goods/services by another method.
Transaction data was invalid or missing required fields.
Cardholder claims they didn't receive the goods/services. Common for physical goods; can apply to services when service didn't occur.
Cardholder claims they cancelled a recurring transaction but was charged anyway. Common for subscription disputes.

reasons.display_name: Visa 13.3 — Not as Described or Defective Merchandise/Services
reasons.description: Cardholder claims the goods/services differed materially from what was advertised.
Cardholder claims the goods received were counterfeit.
Cardholder claims the transaction was misrepresented to them.
Merchant issued a credit that was not processed by the cardholder's bank.
Cardholder cancelled the goods/services but merchant did not refund.

reasons.display_name: Visa 13.8 — Original Credit Transaction Not Accepted
reasons.description: Original credit transaction refused by the cardholder's bank.

reasons.display_name: Visa 13.9 — Non-Receipt of Cash or Load Transaction Value
reasons.description: ATM / prepaid card load didn't complete but was charged.
Cardholder claims the transaction was not authorized. Most common online-fraud chargeback reason for Mastercard.
Card-present chip transaction where merchant didn't use EMV. Similar to Visa 10.1.

reasons.display_name: Mastercard 4863 — Cardholder Does Not Recognize (Potential Fraud)
reasons.description: Cardholder doesn't recognize the transaction; potentially unauthorized use.
Same transaction posted multiple times. Similar to Visa 12.6.1.
Cardholder cancelled recurring; charged anyway.
Transaction presented outside the authorized time window.

reasons.display_name: Mastercard 4853 — Cardholder Dispute of Goods or Services
reasons.description: Broad consumer dispute — goods/services not received, defective, not as described, cancelled.

reasons.display_name: Mastercard 4855 — Goods or Services Not Provided
reasons.description: Cardholder claims goods/services were not provided.
Card-present transaction without required signature.
Cardholder claims the transaction was not authorized. Most common online-fraud Amex chargeback.
Card-absent transaction dispute where cardholder claims non-authorization.
Transaction processed against an unassigned card number.
Transaction amount does not match the authorized amount.
Transaction presented outside the authorized time window.

reasons.display_name: Discover 4855 — Goods or Services Not Provided
reasons.description: Cardholder claims goods/services were not provided.
Similar to Visa 10.4 / Mastercard 4837.
Similar to Visa 10.4 / Mastercard 4837.

notes: dispute_time_limit_days is the CARDHOLDER's time window to file the dispute (from transaction date). Distinct from evidence_deadline_days (merchant's window to respond). Both are informational — the provider webhook communicates the actual evidence_due_by.

reason_category is the CANONICAL grouping (fraud / authorization / processing_errors / consumer_disputes / card_recovery). Reason_code + network are the source of truth; reason_category is derived + frozen.
The catalogue is not exhaustive — some obscure reason codes may fall under 'other' at chargeback creation. Reviewers may extend when new codes appear frequently.
Reason codes evolve — networks update them periodically. This catalogue is versioned via $version + should be reviewed annually against network dispute-guide publications.
